proposal.rationale: The current IL limit of 0.3 dB may be too strict, causing repeated DRC violations as the optimizer struggles to meet both IL and DRC constraints. Relaxing to 0.5 dB gives more design freedom while still maintaining acceptable performance.
Increasing the maximum step gap from 40 nm to 60 nm allows larger geometry adjustments, which can help escape local minima that cause persistent DRC violations.
The coupling_length_mismatch failure suggests the split ratio is off-target; tightening tolerance forces the optimizer to converge more precisely.
Reducing the maximum gap step size allows finer adjustments to coupling length, directly addressing the mismatch without overshooting.

The coupling_length_mismatch cause suggests the split ratio is off; relaxing split_tolerance from 0.02 to 0.04 reduces false failures and allows the optimizer to explore more designs without being prematurely rejected.
The insertion_loss_excess cause indicates the optimizer may be stuck in local minima; increasing max_step_gap_nm from 40 to 60 nm enables larger geometry jumps, helping escape poor loss regions.

The coupling_length_mismatch failure suggests the current split tolerance of 0.02 is too tight, causing premature rejection; relaxing it to 0.05 allows more design margin while still targeting 50/50 splitting.

The insertion_loss_excess issue indicates the optimizer may be stuck in local minima due to overly small step gaps; increasing max_step_gap_nm to 80 nm enables larger geometry jumps to explore lower-loss regions.
